  "findings": [
    "Unpoured seal fitting observed in classified area — fill hole plug visible indicating sealant has not been installed",
    "Seal fitting is incomplete and does not provide required explosionproof seal against passage of gases, vapors, or flames",
    "Conduit system boundary seal in classified location is non-functional without poured sealing compound"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Do not energize circuit until seal is properly poured with approved sealing compound",
    "Remove fill hole plug, pour approved sealing compound per manufacturer instructions, and reinstall plug",
    "Verify seal fitting is accessible and properly filled before scheduling classified-area inspection",
    "Inspect all other seal fittings in the area for similar incomplete pours"
  ],
  "rationale": "In classified (hazardous) locations, NEC 501 and 502 require conduit seals to be filled with approved sealing compound to prevent propagation of flammable atmospheres through the conduit system. An unpoured seal with only the fill hole plug visible is an installed-defective condition — the fitting is physically present but functionally incomplete and non-compliant. This is a critical safety defect because an unsealed conduit run can allow explosive gases to migrate between classified and unclassified areas or between equipment.",
  "references": ["NEC 500.5", "NEC 501.15", "NEC 502.15", "NEC 501.10(B)", "NEC 501.12"]
}
